rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes, and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly whitelisted identifiers
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly exposed as ports or constants
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope for the current requirement
Preserve the original AADL architecture by not modifying component or implementation structures
Use only identifiers that are visible in the current scope or explicitly whitelisted
Avoid assumptions and guarantees that refer to subcomponent ports in component type scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Implementation-level bindings should be placed after subcomponents and connections in implementation scopes
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to subcomponent ports in component type scopes
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are explicitly exposed as ports or constants
